claim.observation: windows.netscan records an ESTABLISHED TCPv4 session (offset 0xbdee9cf0) from 10.10.6.15:59486 to foreign address 10.10.254.1 foreign port 61613, owned by PID 19688 rubyw.exe. The same scan also records an ESTABLISHED TCPv4 session (offset 0xbc1024d0) from 10.10.6.15:49203 to 10.10.200.207:5672 owned by PID 1736 java.exe.
claim.interpretation: rubyw.exe (PID 19688) is the windowless Ruby interpreter; netscan shows it holding a live outbound TCP session from this host to internal host 10.10.254.1 on port 61613. A general-purpose scripting interpreter holding a network session is an artifact worth reviewing, because such interpreters can host either legitimate automation or attacker tooling. Port 61613 is commonly associated with STOMP messaging and the co-resident java.exe session to 10.10.200.207:5672 is commonly AMQP, so the two may belong to a message-bus client stack. This netscan output reflects only the socket endpoints and their live state; it does not reveal the data carried or whether the activity is benign, and is recorded as a lead for corroboration rather than a conclusion.
